name: song_composer
description: Schema for procedural music composition with nested pattern structures

properties.name: tags
properties.description: Song-level mood, energy, and style tags
properties.note: Global tags that influence all parts

properties.name: instrument_list
properties.description: List of instruments used throughout the song
properties.note: Common instruments: kick, snare, closed_hat, open_hat, bass, piano, synth, pad, lead, etc.

properties.name: part_structure
properties.description: High-level song structure defining part sequence

properties.name: parts
properties.description: Dictionary of song parts keyed by name (e.g., 'verse_0', 'chorus_0')

properties.name: part_tags
properties.description: Part-specific energy/density tags (e.g., dense, sparse, energetic, mellow, upbeat, chill)
properties.note: Negative context weights create anti-correlation with previous parts for dynamic contrast

properties.name: duration_bars
properties.description: Duration of this part in bars (measures)
properties.note: Must be satisfied by total length of chord and instrument patterns

properties.name: chords
properties.description: Chord progression for this part using pattern/repetition structure

repetitions.description: Number of times the pattern_structure repeats

patterns.description: Mapping of pattern IDs to chord sequences (e.g., {A: ['I', 'vi', 'IV', 'V'], B: ['I', 'V']})
patterns.note: Each pattern represents bars worth of chords. Total: sum(len(patterns[p]) for p in pattern_structure) × repetitions = duration_bars

properties.name: instruments

repetitions.description: Number of times the pattern_structure repeats

patterns.description: Mapping of pattern IDs to rhythm patterns
patterns.note: Pattern encoding: '1' = event on beat 1 (whole note), '11' = events on beats 1&3 (half notes), '1010' = quarter notes, '10101010' = eighth notes, '1-10' = half note, quarter rest, quarter note. Each pattern = 1 bar. Total: len(pattern_structure) × repetitions = duration_bars

properties.note: Dictionary keyed by instrument name. Each instrument must have patterns totaling duration_bars.

notes: Properties are generated in the order listed
Context references support correlation multipliers: positive values = positive correlation, negative = anti-correlation
parts[-1].* refers to the previous part, parts[-2].* refers to two parts ago
Pattern structures enforce duration constraints: pattern length × repetitions must equal duration_bars
Pattern encoding uses string notation where '1' = note, '0' = rest, '-' = hold/sustain
Part tags use anti-correlation to create dynamic contrast (sparse → dense, mellow → energetic)
